facts_disclosed: Naomi school play May 16, she plays a tree with 3 lines

turns.content: yo sa k pase i need some help figuring out something i got a scheduling thing coming up and i want to make sure i do this right because it matters more than any load ive ever hauled
Hey! Sounds important. What's the scheduling situation? I'll do my best to help you work through it.

okay so my little girl naomi she goes to school up in newark right and they got this play coming up may 16 and she got a part in it man she is playing a tree which i know sounds funny but she actually has 3 lines to say and she has been rehearsing them every single time we facetime she does the voice and everything its the cutest thing you ever seen

That's adorable! A tree with 3 whole lines is a big deal at that age. And the fact that she's been rehearsing on FaceTime with you shows she really wants you to be there. So you need to get May 16 off — is that a Friday or a weekday?

its a friday and see thats the thing fridays are usually heavy dispatch days because everybody wants loads dropped off before the weekend so dispatch always wants me on the road friday but i already went to them and said i need may 16 off period no negotiations i dont care what load is sitting there i am going to be in that auditorium watching my baby be a tree

Good for you for putting your foot down. Dispatch will figure it out — they always do. Have you already put in the formal request or was this more of a verbal heads-up? Sometimes getting it in writing makes all the difference.

yeah i put it in the system and told my dispatcher verbally too his name is rob and he was cool about it he said just make sure you wrap up whatever run youre on by thursday night and i said pa gen pwoblem i will make it work even if i gotta drive 14 hours wednesday to get back

Smart move doing both the system request and the verbal confirmation. And it sounds like Rob is reasonable about it. Just keep an eye on the schedule the week before — if they try to assign you something that would have you 600 miles away on Thursday, flag it early. Do you know what kind of run you'll be on that week?

not yet they usually do the week assignments on monday or tuesday so ill know around may 12 maybe but im going to tell them nothing past virginia that week because i need to be able to get up to newark by thursday evening at the latest i was listening to this podcast about how airline pilots bid for their schedules and i was thinking man truck drivers need something like that you know we just get told where to go

Ha, that's actually a great comparison. Airline pilots have seniority-based bidding systems that let them pick routes and days off — trucking is so far behind on that. Some of the bigger carriers are starting to do preference-based dispatch but it's still pretty rare for OTR drivers.

For the week of May 12, I'd suggest reaching out to Rob again around May 9 or 10 to remind him. Not in a pushy way, just a "hey just confirming I need to be done by Thursday the 15th" kind of thing.

yeah thats a good idea ill hit him up early that week because these dispatchers they got a lot of drivers to manage and they forget stuff and i can not have him forget this one you know this is naomis first real play she was in a christmas thing last year but she was just standing in the back this time she actually has lines to say

There's a big difference between standing in the back and having actual lines. She's going to be looking out into that audience for your face, and knowing you're there is going to mean everything to her. Are you planning to drive up from Atlanta or will your route already have you somewhere in the northeast?

depends on the run that week but worst case i drive marie up from atlanta thats about 13 hours i can do that easy ive done it before i just gotta make sure i get enough sleep first because the last thing i need is to be falling asleep on i-95 at midnight you know bondye bon im gonna be there one way or another

13 hours is a haul but you know that road better than anyone. Just make sure you're rested and give yourself some buffer time — you don't want to be cutting it close with traffic on 95 through the northeast. Are you going to stay in Newark for the weekend or do you have to get right back?

im trying to stay the whole weekend if i can because danielle said naomi can stay with me saturday and we can go do something together maybe go to the park or get ice cream or whatever she wants and then i gotta head back sunday afternoon to be ready for monday dispatch but man even one full day with her is worth it you know i was thinking about recording the play on my phone too so i can watch it when im driving and missing her

That's a perfect plan — the play on Friday, a full Saturday together doing whatever she wants, and recording it so you have it for the road. That recording is going to get you through a lot of late nights at truck stops.

Just a heads-up: check with the school about recording policies. Some schools don't allow phones out during performances, but most have a designated area for parents to film. You don't want to get shushed during her 3 big lines!

Sounds like you've got this figured out. May 16 — tree day. She's going to crush it.
